meaning: vt.期待,预期,盼望,指望,<口>(料)想,认为
examples: Don't expect it to improve overnight.
Don't expect others to read your mind.
Both leaders seemed to expect measurable progress.
Nor do the Klarsfelds expect to be dealing with any further cases of Nazi war criminals.
But you can expect normal, everyday politeness, fair play, communication and consideration from your colleagues.
